title: ranunculus aquatilis var. stagnatilis
intro: Ranunculus aquatilis var. stagnatilis, also known as the pond water-crowfoot or floating water-crowfoot, is a variety of water crowfoot that is characterized by its unique ability to float on the surface of water due to its gas-filled leaves. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly as a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The health benefits of Ranunculus aquatilis var. stagnatilis include its potential to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of a tincture or infusions, although it should be used with caution due to its potential toxicity.
